assertions.description: GPUAdapter exposes a `features` GPUSupportedFeatures set used to detect texture-component-swizzle.
assertions.test: (async()=>{if(!navigator.gpu)return false;const a=await navigator.gpu.requestAdapter();return !!a && a.features && typeof a.features.has==='function';})() instanceof Promise

assertions.description: The string "texture-component-swizzle" is recognised as a GPUFeatureName.
assertions.test: (async()=>{if(!navigator.gpu)return false;const a=await navigator.gpu.requestAdapter();if(!a)return false;try{const dev=await a.requestDevice({requiredFeatures:['texture-component-swizzle']});return !!dev;}catch(e){return /not supported|invalid/i.test(String(e.message||e));}})() instanceof Promise

assertions.description: GPUTextureViewDescriptor dictionary is described by the IDL — runtime check via createView accepting a descriptor.
Swizzle components include 'r','g','b','a','zero','one' (parseable as plain strings in JS).
GPUAdapter.prototype.requestDevice is a function — the swizzle feature is opted in here.
